meta_title: For Founders: client workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Client Workflow to Solve Can't Finish Projects
content.seo_description: Stop stalling your SaaS. Learn a professional client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster and finally finish your MVP.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of rigid, client-focused constraints. By implementing a standardized client workflow, you force scope control and prioritize revenue-generating features. Use Antigravity to scaffold the architecture and Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting, ensuring your MVP reaches the finish line.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 'almost-ready' MVPs. You start with high-octane energy, but as the feature list drifts into scope creep, the momentum evaporates. You aren't failing because you can't code; you're failing because you lack a production-hardened workflow that treats every feature like a deliverable for a paying client.

This isn't about writing more code—it's about building a system that forces you to finish. By treating your own project with the same professional rigor as a high-stakes client engagement, you eliminate the ambiguity that kills motivation. Let's transform your unfinished repository into a market-ready asset.

steps.title: Define the 'Client' Milestone
steps.description: Treat your project as a paid contract. Define one single feature that provides immediate value. If it doesn't solve a problem, delete it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project roadmap and identify the single most impactful feature that validates the core business hypothesis. Provide a scope-limited MVP specification.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my project files and list the top 3 features for an MVP. Create a ticket for the most important one.'

steps.title: Establish the Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate a clean, scalable folder structure that prevents technical debt before you write a single line of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready folder structure for a SaaS MVP using Next.js, Supabase, and Tailwind. Include a clear separation of concerns for API, UI, and Database layers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create the directory structure as specified by the architecture design provided by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Automate the Feature Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to handle the boilerplate, allowing you to focus on the business logic and user experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a technical implementation plan for the authentication module and user dashboard, focusing on security and speed of integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'npm install next-auth --save' && claude run 'Implement the authentication flow based on the technical plan.'

steps.title: Implement the 'Client-Ready' Validation
steps.description: Add testing hooks that simulate user interaction to ensure the feature is actually usable, not just functional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a set of end-to-end test cases for the core user flow, ensuring all edge cases are covered for a production release.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate and run Playwright tests for the user login and dashboard flow.'

steps.title: Deploy to Staging
steps.description: Ship the code. A project is only finished when it is accessible to someone other than you.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a checklist for production deployment, covering environment variables, security headers, and database migrations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Verify environment variables and trigger the deployment script to Vercel.'

pro_tips: Treat your own time as billable: track your hours as if you are charging a client to keep yourself honest.
Implement a 'Kill Switch' for features: if a feature takes more than 4 hours to implement, it is likely too complex for an MVP.
Use Claude Code to write your README documentation concurrently with your code to ensure you don't forget why you built a specific logic.
Prioritize data persistence over UI polish: a working backend with a raw UI is better than a pretty frontend that doesn't save user data.
Always build for the 'Happy Path' first: don't let error handling bloat your project until the core value proposition works.
content.next_steps: Once the MVP is deployed, gather feedback from three potential users and use that data to iterate using the same workflow.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical framework to stop stalling and start shipping. The only difference between a dreamer and a founder is the ability to execute, finish, and deploy. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
